name: University of Cambridge
notes: Cambridge students apply to Engineering (H100) and specialise into Civil, Structural and Environmental Engineering from Year 3. ENGAA required; interview at college. World-class structural mechanics and geotechnical research groups.

name: Imperial College London
notes: UCAS 2026 verified title match. Imperial Civil Engineering has particular strengths in structural engineering, geomechanics and environmental engineering. Strong employer links with Arup, Atkins and Jacobs. No interview required. Mathematics and Physics required at A-level.

name: University of Bristol
notes: UCAS 2026 verified title match. Bristol Civil Engineering is strong in structural dynamics, wind engineering and earthquake engineering. Research partnerships with Arup and Network Rail. Mathematics and Physics required.

name: University of Edinburgh
notes: UCAS 2026 verified title match. Edinburgh's 4-year Honours BEng or 5-year MEng. Strong offshore engineering, coastal engineering and geotechnics (influenced by North Sea industry). Scottish Highers equivalent accepted. Mathematics A-level or Higher required.

name: University of Warwick
notes: UCAS 2026 verified title match. Warwick Civil Engineering emphasises infrastructure systems, geomechanics and sustainable design. Mathematics and Physics required.

name: University of Bath
notes: UCAS 2026 verified title match. Bath Civil Engineering regularly ranks top 5 nationally. Mandatory placement semester with construction and infrastructure employers (Arup, Atkins, Mott MacDonald). Mathematics required.

name: University of Sheffield
notes: UCAS 2026 verified title match. Sheffield Civil Engineering has strong structural, geotechnical and environmental engineering groups. Excellent practical site work component. Mathematics required at A-level.

name: University of Southampton
notes: UCAS 2026 verified title match. Southampton Civil Engineering has particular strength in offshore and coastal engineering, reflecting Southampton's maritime heritage. Strong links to Environment Agency and major infrastructure consultancies.

name: University of Nottingham
notes: UCAS 2026 verified title match. Nottingham Civil Engineering has strong geotechnical and transport engineering groups. Ground Engineering Research Group is nationally recognised. Mathematics required at A-level.

name: University of Manchester
notes: UCAS 2026 verified title match. Manchester Civil Engineering benefits from the city's major infrastructure regeneration (Northern Powerhouse, HS2) providing real case studies. Strong links to Transport for Greater Manchester. Mathematics required.

name: University of Leeds
notes: UCAS 2026 verified title match. Leeds Civil Engineering has strengths in water engineering, transport infrastructure and geotechnics. Excellent industry placement links in Yorkshire. Mathematics required.

name: University of Birmingham
notes: UCAS 2026 verified title match. Birmingham Civil Engineering benefits from proximity to HS2 construction and the Midlands infrastructure corridor. Strong structural and geotechnical research. Mathematics required at A-level.
